entry.title: Re: How can we apply filters on navigation properties of...
entry.content:
Not entirely sure but I think it would something like this:

SELECT.from(EntityA,a => {
            a.field1,
            a.field2,
            a.field3,
            a.toEntityB(b=>{
                b.fieldB1,
                b.fieldB2
            }),
            a.toEntityC(c=>{
                c.fieldC1,
                c.fieldC2
            })
        }).where({ fieldB1:'ValueX' }))

You can also try to write it in sql and parse it:

 const query = cds.parse.cql ( `SELECT a.field1, a.field2, a.field3, a.toEntityB.fieldB1, a.toEntityB.fieldB2, a.toEntityC.fieldC1, a.toEntityC.fieldC2 FROM EntityA as a where a.toEntityC.fieldC2 = '${value}'`)

You can also create a view as described here:

https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/cds/cdl#views-with-inferred-signatures

entry.title: Synchronizing Attributes from Azure to IAS
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I'm encountering an issue with synchronizing certain attributes such as country, department, city, manager etc., from Azure to IAS.

I adding these parameter in IPS  as follows:

aad.user.attributes=id,mail,userPrincipalName,employeeId,displayName,givenName,surname,jobTitle,city,companyName,country,businessPhones,mobilePhone,department

Many Thanks you for any tips.

Best Regards

 

 

 

 

 

{
    "user": {
        "condition": "$.userPrincipalName EMPTY false",
        "mappings": [
            {
                "sourcePath": "$",
                "targetPath": "$"
            },
            {
                "sourcePath": "$.id",
                "targetVariable": "entityIdSourceSystem"
            },
            {
                "sourcePath": "$.mailNickname",
                "targetPath": "$.externalId",
                "optional": true,
                "correlationAttribute": true
            },
            {
                "targetPath": "$.schemas[0]",
                "constant": "urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:core:2.0:User"
            },
            {
                "sourcePath": "$.mail",
                "targetPath": "$.emails[0].value",
                "correlationAttribute": true
            },
            {
                "sourcePath": "$.userPrincipalName",
                "targetPath": "$.userName",
                "correlationAttribute": true
            },
            {
                "sourcePath": "$.displayName",
                "targetPath": "$.displayName",
                "optional": true
            },
            {
                "sourcePath": "$.givenName",
                "targetPath": "$.name.givenName",
                "optional": true
            },
            {
                "sourcePath": "$.surname",
                "targetPath": "$.name.familyName",
                "optional": true
            },
            {
                "sourcePath": "$.mobilePhone",
                "targetPath": "$.phoneNumbers[0].value",
                "optional": true,
                "defaultValue": "888-888-888"
            },
            {
                "targetPath": "$.phoneNumbers[0].type",
                "condition": "$.mobilePhone EMPTY false",
                "constant": "mobile"
            },
            {
                "sourcePath": "$.businessPhones[0]",
                "targetPath": "$.phoneNumbers[1].value",
                "optional": true
            },
            {
                "targetPath": "$.phoneNumbers[1].type",
                "condition": "$.businessPhones.length() > 0",
                "constant": "work"
            },
            {
                "sourcePath": "$.groups",
                "targetPath": "$.groups",
                "optional": true,
                "preserveArrayWithSingleElement": true
            },
            {
                "sourcePath": "$.manager.id",
                "targetPath": "$['urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:extension:enterprise:2.0:User']['manager']['value']",
                "optional": true
            },
            {
                "sourcePath": "$.city",
                "targetPath": "$.addresses[0].locality",
                "optional": true
            },
            {
                "sourcePath": "$.addresses[0].country",
                "targetPath": "$.country",
                "optional": true
            },
            {
                "sourcePath": "$.employeeId",
                "targetPath": "$['urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:extension:enterprise:2.0:User']['employeeNumber']",
                "optional": true
            },
            {
                "sourcePath": "$['resourceExtensions']['urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:extension:enterprise:2.0:User']['department']",
                "targetPath": "$['urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:extension:enterprise:2.0:User']['department']",
                "optional": true
            },
            {
                "sourcePath": "$.manager.displayName",
                "targetPath": "$['urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:extension:enterprise:2.0:User']['manager']['displayName']",
                "optional": true
            }
        ]
    },
    "group": {
        "ignore": false,
        "mappings": [
            {
                "targetPath": "$.schemas[0]",
                "constant": "urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:core:2.0:Group"
            },
            {
                "sourcePath": "$.id",
                "targetVariable": "entityIdSourceSystem"
            },
            {
                "sourcePath": "$.mailNickname",
                "targetPath": "$.externalId",
                "optional": true
            },
            {
                "sourcePath": "$.displayName",
                "targetPath": "$.displayName"
            },
            {
                "sourcePath": "$.members",
                "targetPath": "$.members",
                "optional": true,
                "preserveArrayWithSingleElement": true
            },
            {
                "targetPath": "$.members[*].id",
                "constant": "value",
                "optional": true,
                "type": "rename"
            }
        ]
    }
}

entry.title: Re: Assign default value to field in Fiori Elements
entry.content:
@robmamba25 

The following options are available for prefilling some values in the backend service. Not suitable if you are looking for a way to supply default values from the frontend app, though.

1. NewAction

https://sapui5.hana.ondemand.com/#/topic/11ff444f82e14eb3a2e8eca0065a5055

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/prefilling-fields-when-creating-a-new-entity-with-cap-node-js/ba-p/13593259

2. DefaultValuesFunction

https://sapui5.hana.ondemand.com/#/topic/5ada91cc1ad8455bbfb7e6aee96383f2

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/cap-with-fiori-elements-side-effects-custom-actions-dynamic-expressions/ba-p/13556745 (Concept-2)

entry.title: Re: Issue missing home page for a specific persona in the...
entry.content: After you press OK on that pop-up, the app should continue to work as usual. This is not an error but a warning that you can ignore if you have the personas maintained correctly.

Hi Rajah,

It's difficult to say what is wrong without additional information so please raise a support ticket using the Component ID KM-SEN-NAV and get support involved.  They can take a detailed look at the situation.

Regards
Shane

entry.content:

then have tried to test the connection and getting the same

Any ideia why Im getting invalid handshake? should be automatically once we dont have set up handshake for queue,

entry.title: Re: 0FIAA_SIM values in 0FI_AA_12
entry.content:
Hello Uma,

We have a requirement to mimic S_ALR_87012936 for future years, for example if the report date is 12/31/2028 for a sample company code, this generates simulated depreciation for current year (2024), next (2025), 2026, 2027, 2028  in S4. Can this requirement be satisfied with the help of 0FI_AA_12 extractor via Bex query ?( i see field which is Posted/Simulated value ), but in the periods i can only see until 12, current year 2024.  

Thanks,

Karthik

entry.title: Re: SAP Developer Challenge - Full-Stack - Project Set-up and Database Modeling (Week 1)
Re: Using Smart Links in Book Page Templates
entry.content:
Hi Justin,

The only way to get this to work is to open the Text field of the Text Box insert a link by clicking the yellow + on the dialogue box that appears.  This is still a text link, inside the Text Box, but that resolves correctly.

If you try to insert the smart link through the Link field of the Text Box, it does not resolve correctly and tries to link to the Book Page object, not the Simulation.

You could try creating a support ticket using the Component ID KM-SEN-PRO to get support involved in the conversation and see if there is any workarounds to the issue.

Regards
Shane

entry.title: Re: Message Header in Content modifier SAP CPI
entry.content: You would need to be more specific with your query.

entry.title: Re: Call RAP methods from Control.js
entry.content:
You could use the invokeActions method from the extenstionAPI. See next link from SAP Help:

https://sapui5.hana.ondemand.com/sdk/#/topic/bd2994b69ef542998becbc69ab093f7e 

Regards.

entry.title: Re: Task List with Material VS equipment BOM
entry.content:
Hi, 4HI is Proactive Maintenance, so yes you are correct if you want to, you could just create a General Task List, then add that to the Maintenance Order and that will add all the materials to it in the components tab.

Re. Equipment BOMs and Task Lists, they are different objects with different business use cases.

General Task Lists are typically made with reference to a particular maintenance activity, inspection, repair job (Operations, Documents, Materials etc). And then can be used across many Maintenance Orders if required.

Equipment BOMs can be used to build out the structure of your equipment a bit more and link the spare parts (materials) that are specific to that piece of equipment. This is not shared across other equipment when its setup, its just so that a Maintenance Planner can see the spare parts when they are scoping out a Maintenance Order.

Here are some links in case you want to read up in more detail:

  • Maintenance Task Lists 
  • Maintenance Bills of Material 

Tim

entry.title: Re: Request additional Special G/l indicator in SAP s/4ha...
entry.content: Hi @FakkirswamyKR, does it have an additional cost or is it free? Thank you

entry.title: Re: SAP BUILD APPS is showing subscription error even with booster
entry.content: I have had the same error and have created several accounts, I have tried in incognito mode and in several browsers (Chrome, Edge and Opera) with the same error.

entry.title: Re: Issue with Dynamic Table Access for Copying MOLGA Content Between Tables
entry.content:
V_T52D1 is a maintenance view, not a database table.

Whenever you are doing dynamic code try and run the code that is generated in the program inserted into a test program as-is (not dynamically) to see if it works. By this I mean get the dynamic code from the debugger when you run the program to test it, and then test that code in a temp/test program to see if it compiles and also to see if it works correctly.

I added this to a test program:

"SELECT * FROM v_t52d1 INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_temp) . "

And the syntax check gave me this error:

This makes sense:

I would propose you select from these tables directly using the join condition supplied in the maintenance view within the DDIC.

entry.title: Re: How to Understand the HANA Generated CalculationScenario
entry.content: Correct. IP is passed by HANA in that Calculation Scenario. It won't show up in manage mapping section

entry.title: Video Series - Part 11 - Starting with PDI development
entry.content: This is episode 11 of "Developing side-by-side extensions with CAP for SAP Service Cloud v1" where I am explaining how to test the deployed application through Postman. I will show youhow to prepare and setup the External Web Service Integration and Communication Arrangements in order to obtain the oAuth token, as well as, for calling the REST API endpoint so we can consume them on our next episode.

entry.title: Re: SAC Calculate after aggregation based on datasphere
entry.content:
Use SUM exception aggregation type for GP%, and specify all of the available dimensions as exception aggregation dimensions.

If that does not work, Use SUM exception aggregation type for both GP & GP%, and specify all of the available dimensions as exception aggregation dimensions.

SAP has specifically mentioned about the behavior here 

Aggregations in Formulas | SAP Help Portal

Also check this

Price * Volume Formulas | SAP Help Portal

LMK

-Savio

entry.title: C4C: Adding Additional Filters for Archiving Search
entry.content:
Hi - is there a way to add additional search criteria, like Customer ID when searching archived data via Admin > Archiving > Search functionality?

Currently, we can only search below, but I need to specifically search archived data of a customer

Thanks in advance!

Cheers,.

Marc

entry.title: Re: SAC User Stats - Need a report which provides informa...
entry.content:
Go to the SAC Administration panel: Navigate to Main Menu → System → Monitor → Auditing.
Filter Audit Logs: Use filters to search for specific events such as user logins, changes to stories, models, etc.
You can filter by time range, event type (login, model updates, story updates), and the specific user.

To get the stats on Changes to models, stories, and other content , check Content Lifecycle Management (LCM) logs:

Main Menu → System → Content Network → Manage → Content Updates.

You can also check Monitoring tab in SAC

Main Menu → System → Monitor → Users or Sessions.

The Users tab will show active users, their roles, and last login times. This can be helpful for tracking who is accessing the system. The Sessions tab provides details on user sessions (time logged in, IP addresses, etc.).

entry.title: Re: SAP CAP: req.error inside bound action not highlighti...
entry.content:
A few thoughts:

1. Are you 100% sure that the req.error calls occurs? i.e. Your logic isn't preventing it from getting invoked? 

2. Does this syntax work? 

req.error(400,`The field ${field.description} is mandatory`, `in/${field.mandatoryField}`)

entry.title: Re: SAC Application designer story blended models/lined d...
entry.content:
• Did you Ensure the blended models are correctly linked and configured. Ensure that all dimensions and measures used in the table are correctly bound and mapped to their respective models. If any are missing or unbound, SAC may throw this error.
  • If you've added any custom scripts for clicks or interactions, ensure they correctly reference datasets from the different models.
  • Look for where the getdatasetid is used, and check if it’s properly retrieving the ID. If the model or dataset is not loaded or is null at the time of the click, that would trigger the error.

entry.title: Best approach for updating learning item?
entry.content:
Hey experts, 

I need some opinions on the best way to approach an upcoming situation. 

We will have items that will no longer be available for users as our license will expire. We are working on new content to replace these but they will not be available until 1 month after the license expires. 

I want to understand what would be the best approach here?

Should I deactivate the assignment profiles, then revise the items when they're ready, and reactivate the assignment profiles?

If that's the case, what's the behaviour when we reactivate the assignment profiles?

 

Thank you,

Best,

Ken

entry.title: HANA Smart Data Integration - Data Provisioning Agent connects to Netweaver ABAP in the cloud
entry.content:
Hi community

We're interested with the SAP HANA smart data integration # scenario and we have managed to connect an SAP ECC On-Premise to SAP HANA Cloud using Data Provisioning Agent (DP Agent) running on our private network and ABAP Adapter.

 

We happen to have another SAP Netweaver ABAP but it runs in the cloud. The ABAP system is an SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll # (ECP) running in SAP Data Centre. We're interested to know if we can get the DP Agent to connect to the ECP system

Thank you in advance

Steve

Regards

entry.title: Re: Currency conversion is not functioning incorrect setu...
entry.content:
Dear OwenLiu,

Thank you for your response,

We are posting Post General Journal entries for normal JV transactions (SA) in EUR, considering that our company code currency is HKD, while our client requires the reporting in USD.

As per SAP’s suggestion, we have set USD as the functional currency and have maintained the currency exchange rates for EUR to USD and USD to EUR. However, it seems that the system is converting EUR to HKD first, and then HKD to USD. This has resulted in discrepancies between the reports generated by the client and our SAP reports.

My exchange rates which we have maintained for the January Month.

Specifically, we are posting an amount of EUR 15,420, which should convert into USD 16,808.19 at an exchange rate of 1.09.

However, it appears that the system is improperly converting EUR to HKD at an exchange rate of 8.53, resulting in a value of HKD 131,535.67. Following this, the conversion from HKD to USD is occurring at an exchange rate of 7.81, which leads to a total of USD 16,841.96.

This sequence of conversions deviates from the expected outcome, and as a result, we are seeing a discrepancy of USD 33.77.

The expectation is that the system should convert directly from EUR to USD. Ideally, our company code currency should be USD; however, as per SAP public cloud standards, the country currency by default is set as the company code currency. The users require only the transaction currency to convert to the functional currency, which is intended to be USD for accurate reporting.

Please let me know how we can address this issue moving forward or if there's any additional information you need from my end.

entry.title: Re: Migration of EHANA to Datasphere by leveraging Native HANA Cloud Instance of Datasphere Tenant
entry.content: @VishalM89  As you mentioned the name of our colleague from Max Attention, I assume you have attended the training on SAP Datasphere through an engagement with Max. If this is the case, I will reach out to our colleague and arrange a call involving you, the TQM, and us. During this call, we can discuss the matter in detail. Additionally, we plan to publish a blog post that will cover the migration approach, its limitations, and our suggested approach.

entry.title: Query to list components and unit price
entry.content:
Hi Experts

I am trying to write a query to list all the components in a production order and the unit price from the ITT1 table
The results is multiplying by the number of rows in the BOM. It maybe because there are multiple phantom part numbers in the BOM
Can someone please point me in the right direction

SELECT 
T1.[ItemCode], 
T1.[ItemName], 
T1.[PlannedQty], 
T2.[Price] 
FROM OWOR T0 
INNER JOIN WOR1 T1 ON T0.[DocEntry] = T1.[DocEntry] 
INNER JOIN ITT1 T2 ON T1.[ItemCode] = T2.[Code] 
WHERE T0.[DocNum]  = [%0]

Regards

Rah

entry.title: Re: Vendor Return process

entry.content:

Later, I have extended my CDS view with an entity CDS view(DEFINE VIEW ENTITY) and this has impacted the calculation view where no data returned.

entry.title: Re: SAC Calculate after aggregation based on datasphere
entry.content: Thanks for your reply. Unfortunately, we are using live connection to Datasphere so exception aggregation cant be done at SAC side.

entry.title: Mass close production orders
entry.content:
How to change production order status to closed automatically once the production order status changes to Finished. 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Mass close production orders
entry.content:
When a production order is released, a production lot is created. The planned structure (operations, activities, and reporting points) and the planned data of the production order are copied to the production lot and used as a basis for creating production tasks and default values. 

When the production tasks and, consequently, the production lot are finished, the production order is automatically finished. 

When the production lot is closed, the production order is automatically closed as well. 

Depend on above, you can create a Lot Closure Run to close the mass production lot automatically and ,consequently, the production orders will automatically closed.

Please refer to the documentation for more information. https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_BUSINESS_BYDESIGN/2754875d2d2a403f95e58a41a9c7d6de/2ddeccfe722d101494c086745aa81807.html?version=2202#create-a-lot-closure-run

 

Create a Lot Closure Run steps:

  1. Choose the Lot Closure Run subview.

  2. Click New to open the New Automated Lot Closure Run quick activity.

  3. On the General Data view, enter an ID and a description for the run.

  4. Specify further selection criteria for the run using the tabs available. The system automatically includes or excludes production orders based on these criteria. In the lower table, on each tab page, click Add Row to add a new row.

    • Filter production lots using a selection range

      In the Inclusion/Exclusion field, click the arrow and choose Inclusion or Exclusion. In the Search Pattern field, click the arrow and choose Equals. In the From and To fields, select the start and end range for selection.

    • Filter production lots with reference to a particular selection

      In the Inclusion/Exclusion field, click the arrow and choose Inclusion or Exclusion. In the Search Pattern field, click the arrow and choose the appropriate operator, for example Less than or Equal To. In the From field, select the business document for the selection, for example Product ID 8186.

    Repeat the steps outlined above to specify the selection criteria for each relevant tab.

  5. At the top of the New Automated Lot Closure Run quick activity, click Save. Then click Close to return to the Automated Actions view.

    Note

    You can choose to activate the run from here, or you can activate the run later, just before scheduling. Click Set to Active to activate the run from here. To activate the run later, select the run you want to activate. Click Actions and then choose Set to Active.

    The new lot closure run has been created and saved to the system. You can now define a schedule for this run.

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hello @David_Chaviano 

Thank you for your email and for the opportunity to participate in SAP Learning’s Digital Skills Initiative. I am very interested in this program, as I have been out of work for a few months and am eager to transition into the technology industry. While my background is in procurement, I am enthusiastic about learning how SAP can be effectively applied in the workplace.

I believe I am a strong fit for the Digital Skills Initiative due to my dedication, adaptability, and passion for technology. I am committed to learning new skills and excited about starting a career in this field.

I have already created an SAP account (SAP ID: vikram1231) and joined the Beginner Corner. I’ve also read the blog post on "learning SAP" and am eager to begin my studies. Specifically, I am focusing on the learning journey for Implementing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Sourcing and Procurement, as I believe this certification will greatly enhance my career prospects.

I am confident in my ability to complete the Digital Skills Initiative successfully and earn my SAP certification. I am a fast learner and willing to put in the extra effort required to master the material.

However, I would like to mention that my current financial situation is challenging, as I come from a small village in India and have been struggling to secure employment. I am hopeful that this initiative will provide me with the skills I need to turn my career around.

I am extremely grateful for the opportunity to be a part of this program and look forward to getting started. I look forward to hearing from you soon.

Warm regards,
Baskar K
SAP ID: vikram1231

entry.title: Can we delete client 001 and 066 in solution Manager 7.2
entry.content:
Can client 001 and 066 in Solution Manager 7.2 be deleted?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Can we delete client 001 and 066 in solution Manager 7.2
entry.content:
Yes client 001 and 066 can be deleted. For further details please refer to below SAP KBA/Note.

1897372 - EarlyWatch Mandant 066 - Can Client 066 be deleted?

1749142 - How to remove unused clients including client 001 and 066

entry.title: Customer Consignment Process
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

We are using SAP with Extended Warehouse System and when I was trying to create a Customer Consignment process, I was not able to see the warehouse task created under Run Outbound Process - Deliveries app in SAP S/4 HANA Fiori.

Wondering, is there any configuration needs to be done for customer consignment with EWM? EWM supports all consignment fill up, issue, return and pickup process?

Please throw some lights on the above.

Thanks! SAP S/4HANA SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Sales EWM - Warehouse Order Processing SD (Sales and Distribution) EWM - Delivery Processing EWM - Shipping and Receiving

entry.title: Re: SAP Datasphere is ready to take over the role of SAP BW
entry.content:
Throughout my career I have also exclusively worked with my clients that were using BW, from version 3.x to 7.x and now BW/4HANA 2021.

These companies have strong in-house BW developers and also BW freelancers (which they pay a lot of $$ to). These companies also do not have any AI / ML use-cases, it is usually the good ol' enterprise reporting on Finance, Manufacturing data etc. Then they will use Analysis for Office or SAC for reports. For them, they do not see any value with moving from BW to Datasphere yet.

If anyone here has some good experience after implementing Datasphere to replace or running side by side with BW, do share so that my heart can be more open to Datasphere.

entry.title: Re: What is the best practice for storing attachment files that were uploaded from RAP-Fiori applica
entry.content: Are there anyway to link attachment file to BTP DMS?

entry.title: Screen field value wasn't passed into search help import parameter
entry.content:
Hi experts, 

For the following material batch search help, I can't get material number (MATNR), which was input in the screen, in the search help function exit. What could be reason of the issue? 

Field MATNR and CHARG (batch) are in the same screen structure.

 

Regards,
Eric

entry.title: Output condition records in T.codes: VV11, VV21, and VV31. Please any standard BAPI,F.M,Class?
entry.content:
We have a requirement for a mass upload functionality(File upload) for output condition records in T.codes: VV11, VV21, and VV31. Unfortunately, we have not been able to find a suitable BAPI, function module, or class for this purpose.

If you have any F.M / class, kindly please share with details,

entry.title: How to delete data from tables
entry.content:
How to delete data or reduce table from those tables. 

There are 3 big tables in one of my Solution Manager

  1. BALDAR  12G+
  2. REPOLOAD 10G+
  3. DSVASRESULTSGEN 7.5G+ 

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to delete data from tables
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

To reproduce the size for the mentioned large tables, they should be processed by different components as they belong to different areas. Please refer to the following KBA to process these tables accordingly and if further support is required from SAP, please create cases for them at the correct component separately.

2388483 - How-To: Data Management for Technical Tables 

Best regards,
Ellie
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: Open Task Button is not working from BTP workzone standard edition - CF
entry.content: Hi @srinivass, I've similar requirements, is there any resolution on this?

entry.title: Re: Connecting BPC to External ERP System (SAGE)
entry.content:
It depends on version of SAP BW & BPC. Based on my experience, BODS ( BO Data Services) is the best middleware to connection BPC with non-BPC. BODS is a middleware with heterogeneous sources and applications.

When you search for such document, you have to Seach for integration of SAP BW (not BPC) since SAP BW is primary application. BPC is just add-on to SAP BW.

Some links for you.

How to integrate SAP ECC and external non-SAP data sources in BPC (column5.com)

http://help.sap.com/bods

http://help.sap.com/businessobject/product_guides/sboDS41/en/sbo41_ds_is_master_en.pdf

entry.title: bapi_po_change issue in looping when mass deleting the BOM components
entry.content: Hello everyone, I'm having a problem when using the BAPI_PO_CHANGE when deleting the BOM components inside the loop. the first line is deleted but when the second line hits, it says "no data changed". can anybody know what is the problem? I'm stuck on this one.  If there is a solution, may I know how?

entry.title: Re: ABAP Test Cockpit in the Cloud - What is already possible
entry.content:
Hi Olga,

Thanks for sharing such handy articles, it's really helped my team and I on the setup for our S/4 HANA systems.

I do have a question though, might you know if this central ATC checks on BTP works on ECP(Employee Central Payroll) which is a public cloud edition of an 740 ABAP system?

Regards,

Chris

entry.title: Re: Asset Transation type derivation
entry.content: Please post how it is resolved ?

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to integrate a sapui5 BarcodeScanner i...
entry.content: Hi @gregorw, I completely understood now. As u said I mistaken CAPM as an alternative/advanced way of developing Full-Stack FIORI APP. But I understood now that CAPM is complete backend process for applications but it comes with an extra capability of providing UI Elements with OData Annotations. I got it now that we should use SAPUI5/CAPM based on business requirement & feasibility of solution. Thank you so much for helping me..... 😊🙌👍

entry.title: Re: Devtoberfest 2024 Contest
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I recently joined the Devtoberfest Group and entered my SAP Community ID in the gameboard. However, I received the following message:

“Although you have entered a valid SAP Community ID and may have earned some Devtoberfest badges, it appears that you haven’t registered for Devtoberfest yet. Registration is required to accept the legal terms and conditions, which is necessary for receiving any prizes. Registrations are uploaded in batches, so it could just be a delay, but please check or register again via the SAP Community Group.”

Has anyone else encountered this issue or know how long it takes for registrations to update?

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Re: SAP Business One Mobile application with Single Sign...
entry.content:
Hi PhoebeYeo 

If you are using an on-demand solution, or if you are using an on-premise solution and have
enabled SSO,  please define the value of the Bind with Microsoft Windows Account field in the Users - Setup window in SAP Business One.

If SSO is working with business one client or browser access, it will also work with mobile app.

Thank You.

Kind Regards
Nidhi Singh
SAP Business One Support

entry.title: Re: gCTS is still here
entry.content:
Hi Taryck,

CALM doesn't offer any integration with Git for ABAP. Therefore, you can only work with the CTS  - so Transport Requests as they are. There is no option for reverting.

Kind regards
karin

entry.title: Re: TMS - Transport SAP Cloud Integration (CI/CPI) Content with Transport Management Service (TMS) a
entry.content:
@Vnekat I am responsible for Content Agent service. You mentioned that the configuration check was successful that hints that destinations are created correctly. Could you pls confirm if the role "WorkspacePackagesTransport" is assigned to service instance when you created a service instance of Process Integration Runtime (it-rt), plan (api) in your Source subaccount?
Pls feel free to create a support ticket in queue "BC-CP-LCM-CAS". 
Alternatively you can also try to use Content Agent UI to transport Integration Packages, it provides more details on help on the error details. Here are 2 blogs that describe the using Content Agent Service. 
New User Interface for Content Agent Service
How to use SAP Content Agent Service (Video)

entry.title: Re: B1 Database Migration SQL >> Hana 9.3 pl14
entry.content:
Hi Krish_SAP

Please check SAP note 3061127 for the issue faced.

Thank You.

Kind Regards
Nidhi Singh
SAP Business One Support

entry.title: Re: how to check 0equipment attr and Text data in hana vi...
entry.content:
First, you need to click the checkbox  "Generate Hana views" in 0EQUIMENT infoobject.

For time-dependent master data, the generated view will be in the format: /<SID>/IOBJNM_TD

<SID> is your BW system identifier (Check with Basis team for this ID).
IOBJNM is the technical name of your InfoObject. 0equipment

If BW system SID is BW1, the HANA view for time-dependent master data will be   /BW1/0equiment_TD

BTW, Text tables are never time-dependent.

Furthermore, in some BW versions, you can check if the views have been successfully generated in SAP BW , Go to Transaction RSRTS_ODP_DIS. Enter the InfoObject name and check if the HANA view is listed there.

entry.title: Re: Error Message: Message no. UDM_BUPA_MSG060 in BP Master Data - Collection Profile tab
entry.content: Any update from SAP on this issue

entry.title: Re: Unable to proceed on MDK Annotations
entry.content: I'm following the same documentation and I'm facing the same issue too. Did you find any fix? @ABMetallica?

entry.title: Re: Difference b/w these subquery and inner join? I am ge...
entry.content:
I expect there are some duplicates within your result. If you have some results with different tab1.bb (= tab2.bb_id) you will get more entries for each of them.

With the subquery you will have

... where tab1.bb in (..., value1,value2, ...)

This makes one record for each combination of tab1 and score. For the inner join you will get a record for value1 and one for value2 for each of these combinations.

If you are interested in the different result values without counting them, you can use select distinct or delete the duplicates after the selection.

sort lt.
delete adjacent duplicates from lt.

entry.title: Re: SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) Intern
entry.content: We have made a connection on SAC with SF and the thing that we want to do is that we want to bring our SF data in SAC but when we are loading a dataset or doing other things but we our data is not be showing in SAC can you help me what can we do.

entry.title: When come to an end, the unknown task occurred during the SAP B1 version upgrade..
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

 

I've been upgrading the sap b1 on mssql version again, from 8.81 to 10.0, since 5:00 AM yesterday.

When come to an end, there is unknown task working. I don't know what this task doing but I see that it replicated the primary data file of the database with difference extension, The file name format is [database name].mdf_MSSQL_DBCC12.

 

this is very very annoying and not professional. It will take so much time. It is been 29 hours and the upgrade has not finished yet.

What is this task doing now ? does sap ever know this ? The customer is really wasting time and the lead will not choose sap b1 instead they will buy another erp product.

when I open the sap b1 choose company window, the company database is upgraded

can the user use sap b1 now ? have anyone experienced this unknown task ? how long was it took place ?

Rgds,

Jimmy

entry.title: Re: Auto increase the .ldf database size SAP Business One...
entry.content:
Hi @nidhisingh14 

It's not for SBO-COMMON database. It is increase the size of other database except than SBO-COMMON.

Regards,

Nikunj

entry.title: Re: Query to list components and unit price
entry.content:
Hi 

you can try this

/*Getting all lines from Production Order*/
/*as shown in the grid*/
/*by OWOR.DocEntry*/

DECLARE @Stage AS nvarchar(15)
DECLARE @Row AS INT = 0

/*Add OWOR.DocEntry here*/
DECLARE @DocEntry AS  INT = 22275

/*Check if TempTable already exist*/
IF OBJECT_ID ('tempdb..#Lines','U') IS NOT NULL
/*Else*/
DROP TABLE [dbo].[#Lines]
/*Create new Table*/
CREATE TABLE [#Lines]
(
	[Typ] nvarchar(max),
	[Code]  nvarchar(max),
	[Dscrption]  nvarchar(max),
	[VisOrder] nvarchar(max),
	[LineNum]  nvarchar(max)
)
/*Check for Stages*/
IF 
(
	SELECT
		TOP 1
		[StageID]
	FROM 
		[WOR4]
	WHERE
		[WOR4].[DocEntry] = @DocEntry
)
is not null

BEGIN
/*Set Cursor and get all Stages*/
DECLARE [StageCursor] CURSOR
FOR
	SELECT
		[StageID]
	FROM 
		[WOR4]
	WHERE
		[WOR4].[DocEntry] = @DocEntry
	ORDER BY 
		[WOR4].[StgEntry] ASC
;

/*Start Cursor*/
OPEN [StageCursor]
;

FETCH NEXT FROM [StageCursor] INTO 
    @Stage

WHILE @@FETCH_STATUS = 0
    BEGIN
INSERT INTO [#Lines] ([Typ],[Code],[Dscrption],[VisOrder],[LineNum]) 
	/*Getting Stages*/
	SELECT 
		  'Routenabschnitt'
		, [ORST].[Code]
		, ''
		, ''
		, ''
	FROM 
		[WOR4]
		INNER JOIN [ORST] ON 
			[ORST].[AbsEntry] = [WOR4].[StageId]
	WHERE
		[WOR4].[DocEntry] = @DocEntry 
			AND 
				[WOR4].[StgEntry] = @Stage

INSERT INTO [#Lines] ([Typ],[Code],[Dscrption],[VisOrder],[LineNum])
	/*Getting Lines according to Stage*/
	SELECT
		  CASE WHEN [WOR1].[ItemType] = -18 THEN 'Text'
		       WHEN [WOR1].[ItemType] = 290 THEN 'Ressource'
			   WHEN [WOR1].[ItemType] = 4 THEN 'Artikel'
		  ELSE 'Unbekannt' END AS [Typ]
		, CASE WHEN [WOR1].[ItemType] = -18 THEN '' ELSE [WOR1].[ItemCode] END AS [Code]
		, CASE WHEN [WOR1].[ItemType] = -18 THEN [Wor1].[LineText] 
		       WHEN [WOR1].[ItemType] = 290 THEN [ORSC].[ResName]
			   WHEN [WOR1].[ItemType] = 4 THEN [OITM].[ItemName]
		  ELSE 'Unbekannt' END AS [Dscrption]
		, [WOR1].[VisOrder]
		, [WOR1].[LineNum]
	FROM
		[WOR1]
	LEFT JOIN [OITM] ON 
		[OITM].[ItemCode] = [WOR1].[ItemCode]
	LEFT JOIN [ORSC] ON 
		[ORSC].[VisResCode] = [WOR1].[ItemCode]
	WHERE 
		[WOR1].[DocEntry] = @DocEntry 
			AND 
				[WOR1].[StageId] = @Stage
	ORDER BY 
		[WOR1].[VisOrder]

			FETCH NEXT FROM [StageCursor] INTO 
				@Stage;
		END;

CLOSE [StageCursor];

DEALLOCATE StageCursor;
END;
ELSE
/*If there are no Stages*/
BEGIN
INSERT INTO [#Lines] ([Typ],[Code],[Dscrption],[VisOrder],[LineNum])
	SELECT
		  CASE WHEN [WOR1].[ItemType] = -18 THEN 'Text'
		       WHEN [WOR1].[ItemType] = 290 THEN 'Ressource'
			   WHEN [WOR1].[ItemType] = 4 THEN 'Artikel'
		  ELSE 'Unbekannt' END AS [Typ]
		, CASE WHEN [WOR1].[ItemType] = -18 THEN '' ELSE [WOR1].[ItemCode] END AS [Code]
		, CASE WHEN [WOR1].[ItemType] = -18 THEN [Wor1].[LineText] 
		       WHEN [WOR1].[ItemType] = 290 THEN [ORSC].[ResName]
			   WHEN [WOR1].[ItemType] = 4 THEN [OITM].[ItemName]
		  ELSE 'Unbekannt' END AS [Dscrption]
		, [WOR1].[VisOrder]
		, [WOR1].[LineNum]
	FROM
		[WOR1]
	LEFT JOIN [OITM] ON 
		[OITM].[ItemCode] = [WOR1].[ItemCode]
	LEFT JOIN [ORSC] ON 
		[ORSC].[VisResCode] = [WOR1].[ItemCode]
	WHERE 
		[WOR1].[DocEntry] = @DocEntry 
	ORDER BY 
		[WOR1].[VisOrder]
END

/*Getting Data - Showing Lines as in Production Order*/
SELECT 
  ROW_NUMBER () OVER (ORDER BY (select 0)) AS [#]
, * 
FROM [#Lines]
DROP TABLE [#Lines]

regards Lothar

entry.title: Single User Performance Measurements for API using JMeter, SUPA, IPA in a Jenkins Piper Pipeline
entry.content:
Introduction:

In this tutorial, we will walk through the process of using JMeter and SUPA for performance measurements, IPA for reporting and Jenkins for automation of the testing process and reporting. By the end of this tutorial, you will be able to set up the piper method in Jenkins that automates performance measurement, uses SUPA to take measurements, and uploads the results to IPA for reporting.

Prerequisites:

  • A basic understanding of JMeter
  • Access to a Jenkins server and a working piper pipeline
  • Access to IPA

Configuration

Get SUPA configurations

  1. Download and Install SUPA following the steps mentioned here.
  2. Once installed, open SUPA Application.
  3. Click on Config   
  4. Check the "Upload measurement result to IPA" checkbox and fill in your IPA username and password and click Next.

  5. If a project already available under which the measurements are required to be uploaded then select "Unmanaged Projects" and find it using the dropdown, else create a new project by selecting the "Create new Unmanaged Project" button and giving it a name. Click Next.

  6. Enter the Release in the textbox again "Select/Type Release" and press Enter. Similarly fill Scenario and Variant fields. Change Rule Set using drop down menu. You can explore the KPIs by clicking Show KPIs after selecting a Rule Set.(Note: These changes are temporary and can be changed later during configuring pipeline). Click Next.

  7. Click Yes.

  8. Click Next until you reach the components screen.

  9. In this screen drag and drop the components you need in your testing. For example, here HANA and Generic client are used. Each component has to be configured separately.

  10. Once configured, click Next until you reach end Screen.

  11. Finally click on "Save and Quit" button to save the configuration as .properties file.

  12. These steps will generate 2 files.

    1. A .properties file which contains all the configuration made. This can be found at the path used during saving the configuration in previous step.

    2. A keystore file which contains all the credentials used during configuration. This Keystore file is encrypted and referenced in the .properties file. This can be found in the user home folder (Windows: %USERPROFILE% , Linux: $HOME). More information can be found here.

  13. The .properties file would look like:
    1. The cmid will be generated and will correspond to the Keystore file.
    2. Other configuration can be changed as per requirement.

Configure JMeter Test Plan

  1. Download the JMeter test plan template which uses SUPA Proxy from here. (Note: Other test plan templates along with their usage can found here.)
  2. The Test Plan will look like this.

     

  3. First comes a Once Only Controller which has an HTTP Request to reset the data providers. (here: "SUPA_resetDataProviders"). (Note: This section can also be used to set any thread level variable used by any subsequent threads like access_token from an authorisation http request.)
  4. Then comes Simple controller (here "API_TEST_CASE_1") will contain "SUPA_start" (HTTP Request for starting SUPA measurement) followed by an HTTP Request (here "HTTP Request Test 1") which calls the API. Finally another HTTP Request for stopping SUPA Measurement ("SUPA_stop"). This can be replicated to add more Test cases
  5. Finally an If Controller is used to check the number of iteration completed and is used to generate report and upload to IPA.
  6. The "User Defined Variables" section under the thread group is used to define the thread level variables which can also take values from the Jenkins Piper method.

Configure Jenkins Pipeline

  1. sapSUPAExecuteTests piper method is used to run the performance test in a Jenkins pipeline.

  2. This required 3 files to be present in the repository.
    1. The Keystore file generated earlier (by default named "supa.secstore.jceks") to be present in the root of the repository.
    2. The .properties file generated earlier.
    3. The .jmx file corresponding to the JMeter Test Plan.
  3. The piper method can be used as below:  

     

    scriptType: here jmeter is used. More can be explored here.
    1. testRepository: link to the GitHub repository
    2. testRepositoryBranch: branch in the repository to reference and find the 3 files mentioned above.
    3. githubTokenCredentialsId: GitHub token stored as Secret Text in Jenkins Credentials store.
    4. supaConfig: relative path to .properties file from root of the test repository.
    5. testJMeterScript: relative path to .jmx file from root of the test repository.
    6. envVars: list of environment variables. All variables that are required to be propagated to jmeter test plan has to be prefixed with "J_". (Example: J_IPA_RELEASE is being passed to jmeter test plan as IPA_RELEASE)

IPA Reporting

The reports will be uploaded to IPA server and can be consumed by logging on to the IPA Dashboard.

These results can also be downloaded as excel sheet or visualised as graphs in the IPA Dashboard.

 

Conclusion

By following this tutorial, you have learned how to set up a pipeline in Jenkins that automates performance testing using JMeter, captures measurements using SUPA, and uploads the results to IPA for reporting. This automated process can help streamline your performance testing efforts and provide valuable insights into the performance of your applications. Happy testing!

Resources

  1. sapSUPAExecuteTests

  2. Performance tools Workzone Page
  3. JMeter templates and configurations
  4. IPA FAQ
  5. SUPA Documentation

entry.title: Re: Difference b/w these subquery and inner join? I am ge...
entry.content:
The subquery method only matches rows where tab1.bb is in the list of BB_ID values produced by the subquery. The subquery does not combine conditions between table1 and table2 directly but applies filtering in stages.
Join logic directly combine rows based on match conditions. Incorrect or additional joins can produce more rows or incorrect matches.

To replicate the subquery logic with joins, you should ensure that the joins and filtering conditions align with the logic in the subquery. Here’s how you can structure it:


SELECT tab1.act_id
FROM table1 AS tab1
INNER JOIN :it_score AS score
ON score.id = tab1.id
INNER JOIN table2 AS tab2
ON tab1.bb = tab2.BB_ID
AND score.id = tab2.id
WHERE tab2.si = :iv_si;

entry.title: Reading .XLS file (Excel 2003) in background
entry.content:
Good morning, I have been going around for several days with this issue of processing an .XLS file from the background, and with the file hosted on the application server, and I have not found a way that suits my requirement, and my client's partner is not willing to convert the file to a format such as .CSV or .XLSX, which can be processed from the background.

At the moment, the only thing that is working for me is the execution of a python script, called from my ABAP code, but I have some concerns, and probably in the end this method will not be accepted. In any case, I leave my idea here, and I would like, if you would be very kind, to help me to clear up my doubts about whether there is some security problem that I am not considering, or some other that could become an inconvenience. Thank you very much in advance.

 

The requirements are:
  • python3
  • pip3
  • pandas
  • xlrd
The flow would be to execute from the ABAP program, a command configured in transaction SM69, which will have as parameters the script path and the .XLS file path. This will execute the script that will take the file path, convert it to .CSV, and if everything goes well, it will save the .CSV file in the same path. After executing the script, we continue in the ABAP program processing the converted .CSV file, and continue with the process.
 

The following steps were reproduced on a miniSAP test system:

Configuring transaction SM69

This transaction executes commands as if it were the server terminal. In the “Operating System Command” parameter we put python3 (or python depending on the version(s) installed on the server), and in “Parameter for Operating System Command” we would put the parameters that would be executed with the call to python3, in our case the path of the script, and separated by a space, the path of the .XLS file. In our case we will leave this blank, but if we wanted to test the configuration from the SM69 we could fill in that field to be able to execute it right there. We also check the “Additional Parameters Allowed” checkbox.

Call from ABAP Code:

In the following ABAP report we execute the command previously configured in SM69 with the FM `SXPG_STEP_COMMAND_START`, we pass the script path and .XLS file path parameters, and if OK, we look for the CSV file created in the script to read and process it.

In my case, the created file did not have read permissions, so I had to create a new command in SM69 to change the permissions and be able to read them, but this depends on how the server is configured, it may not be necessary.

 

*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*& Report YTEST_XLS_READ
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*&
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
REPORT ytest_xls_read.

PARAMETERS: p_ok type string.

DATA lv_parameters(255) TYPE c.
DATA lv_chmod(255)    TYPE c.
DATA lv_xls_file      TYPE string.
DATA lv_csv_file      TYPE string.
DATA lv_script_file   TYPE string.
DATA: lt_log          TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF btcxpglog.
DATA: lv_line         TYPE string.

lv_xls_file = '/usr/sap/NPL/D00/data/BigTable.xls'. "Supuesta dirección del XLS
lv_csv_file = '/usr/sap/NPL/D00/data/BigTable.csv'. "Supuesta dirección del CSV
lv_script_file = '/usr/sap/NPL/D00/data/reader.py'. "Supuesta dirección del script

"Concatenamos los parametros que deseamos, en nuestro la ruta del script y la del archivo XLS
lv_parameters = |{ lv_script_file } { lv_xls_file }|.

START-OF-SELECTION.
CALL FUNCTION 'SXPG_STEP_COMMAND_START'
  EXPORTING
    commandname                = 'ZXLS_TO_CSV'
    additional_parameters      = lv_parameters
    operatingsystem            = sy-opsys "Linux
    stdincntl                  = 'R'
    stdoutcntl                 = 'M'
    stderrcntl                 = 'M'
    tracecntl                  = '0'
    termcntl                   = 'C'
  TABLES
    log                        = lt_log "Log que luego podemos iterar para mostrar por SPOOL
  EXCEPTIONS
    command_not_found          = 1
    parameter_expected         = 2
    parameters_too_long        = 3
    security_risk              = 4
    wrong_check_call_interface = 5
    no_permission              = 6
    unknown_error              = 7
    communication_error        = 8
    system_error               = 9
    cannot_get_rfc_dests       = 10
    job_update_failed          = 11
    job_does_not_exist         = 12
    program_start_error        = 13
    too_many_parameters        = 14
    OTHERS                     = 15.
IF sy-subrc <> 0.
ELSE.

"Paso OPCIONAL, en el caso de que el archivo generado no tenga permisos de lectura
  lv_chmod = |777 { lv_csv_file }|.
  CALL FUNCTION 'SXPG_STEP_COMMAND_START'
    EXPORTING
      commandname                = 'ZCHMOD'
      additional_parameters      = lv_chmod
      operatingsystem            = sy-opsys
      stdincntl                  = 'R'
      stdoutcntl                 = 'M'
      stderrcntl                 = 'M'
      tracecntl                  = '0'
      termcntl                   = 'C'
    TABLES
      log                        = lt_log
    EXCEPTIONS
      command_not_found          = 1
      parameter_expected         = 2
      parameters_too_long        = 3
      security_risk              = 4
      wrong_check_call_interface = 5
      no_permission              = 6
      unknown_error              = 7
      communication_error        = 8
      system_error               = 9
      cannot_get_rfc_dests       = 10
      job_update_failed          = 11
      job_does_not_exist         = 12
      program_start_error        = 13
      too_many_parameters        = 14
      OTHERS                     = 15.


"Lectura del CSV para luego procesarlo como sea necesario
  IF sy-subrc IS INITIAL.
    OPEN DATASET lv_csv_file FOR INPUT IN TEXT MODE ENCODING DEFAULT.
    DO 10 times.
      IF sy-subrc IS INITIAL.
        READ DATASET lv_csv_file INTO lv_line.
        WRITE lv_line.
      ELSE.
        EXIT.
      ENDIF.
    ENDDO.
    CLOSE DATASET lv_csv_file.
  ENDIF.
ENDIF.

 

Finally I attach my python script that will be executed and convert my .XLS file to .CSV

 

import os
import sys

import pandas as pd


def convert_xls_to_csv(xls_file_path):
    try:
        # Verifica si el archivo .xls existe
        if not os.path.exists(xls_file_path):
            print(f"El archivo {xls_file_path} no existe.")
            return

        # Verifica que el archivo tenga extensión .xls
        if not xls_file_path.endswith(".xls"):
            print("El archivo no tiene la extensión .xls.")
            return

        # Lee el archivo .xls
        xls_data = pd.read_excel(xls_file_path, engine="xlrd")

        # Genera la ruta del archivo .csv (misma ruta que el .xls)
        csv_file_path = xls_file_path.replace(".xls", ".csv")

        # Convierte el archivo a formato .csv
        xls_data.to_csv(csv_file_path, index=False)

        print(f"El archivo se ha convertido y guardado como {csv_file_path}.")
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"Ocurrió un error al convertir el archivo: {str(e)}")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    # Verifica que se haya pasado un argumento (ruta del archivo .xls)
    if len(sys.argv) != 2:
        print("Uso: python3 script.py /ruta/al/archivo/BigTable.xls")
    else:
        # Toma el archivo .xls desde el parámetro pasado
        file_path = sys.argv[1]
        convert_xls_to_csv(file_path)

 

I find it simple, but it works, it can convert the file from the background. I appreciate comments on security issues that I may not be considering, technical issues, and anything else that can help me improve.

Thank you very much

entry.title: Re: Time Account Payouts/Leave Cashout - Whats New for Payouts in EC Time in b2005!!
entry.content:
Hi TM Expertise,

 

I have scenario in PEP rule with Time Payout , 

max limit in account to CF - 70.

Current year max limit for CF is 15 or less.

So balance will CF from Previous year CF and above that it will be encashed. 

Please help me with rule.

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Unable to proceed on MDK Annotations
entry.content: No @MonishKanna. Still waiting on the revert. Hoping from a revert from @bill_froelich soon.

entry.title: How to use XSUAA in FastAPI Python app
entry.content:
Hi there, 

I have a fastapi app with 2 routes, one for rendering an html page and one for serving a request. I am using Jinja2 for templating.

Example of one of the route:

@router.get("/", response_class=HTMLResponse)
async def read_root(request: Request):
    return templates.TemplateResponse("index.html", {"request": request})

My manifest.yaml for deploying it to cloud foundry:

---
applications:
- name: fastapi-app
  disk_quota: 2048M
  memory: 256M
  path: ./
  routes:
    -   route: fastapi-app.cfapps.eu10.hana.ondemand.com
  buildpacks:
  - python_buildpack
  command: uvicorn com.crack.snap.make.app:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port $PORT
  services:
    - app-xsuaa
    - app-logging-service
  logging:
    level: error
  env:
    PYTHONUNBUFFERED: true
    xsuaa_connectivity_instance_name: "app-xsuaa"
    xsuaa_destination_instance_name: "app-xsuaa"

How do I protect these fastapi routes directly using XSUAA, without having to create one more webapp then use app-router and then forwarding the request to fastapi app?

Also I want the fastapi to use the sub-account's default authentication which we do by using redirect-url of xs-security.json

{
	"xsappname": "fastapi-app",
	"tenant-mode": "dedicated",
	"scopes": [{
		"name": "$XSAPPNAME.fastapi_scope"
	}],
	"role-templates": [{
		"name": "FastAPIRoleTemplate",
		"default-role-name": "FastAPIRole",
		"description": "Role template for app users",
		"scope-references": ["$XSAPPNAME.fastapi_scope"]
	}
	],
	"oauth2-configuration": {
		"redirect-uris": [
			"https://*.cfapps.eu10.hana.ondemand.com/**"
		]
	}
}

Any help on achieving this will be really appreciated, we can also have a blog post on the same topic

SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry runtime and environment Python SAP BTP Security

entry.title: Updating Extension Field with External Hook in SAP Service Cloud Version 2
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I am working on updating an extension field called zWebSite in SAP, where I want to store the website obtained from the Organizational Unit entity using ExtenalHook PreHook. To achieve this, I developed a small application in SAP BTP. However, I am encountering an issue with the following error:

Custom logic error: FAILED_PRECONDITION: Error raised from the external system with HTTP Status Code: 500 "Request failed with status code 422".

My code(JS):

I am wondering about the correct structure for the update. Specifically, I would like to know:

Is it necessary to send the entire currentImage data in the update request, or is it sufficient to only send the extension field zWebSite?

I appreciate any help or suggestions you can provide.

Thank you!

entry.title: Re: Enable Technical Help option on right click in webdynpro abap application
entry.content: Based on the provided hint, I have identified the root cause of TM apps in S/4HANA 2023. Please refer to KBA 3518064 for more information.

entry.title: Re: can we create a sales contract using a reference cont...
entry.content:
Hi,

We have a sales contract created for sales area (Sorg:1000, Dist Chl 10, Div 01) here Dist Chl 10 is for sales and the warranty contract for sales area  (Sorg:1000, Dist Chl 20, Div 01) here Dist Chl 20 is for services. And the Sales doc types (sales contract Z$$1 & warranty contract Z&&1)are assigned for the respective sales areas. Now we want to create a warranty contract "Z&&1" with reference to sales contract Z$$1. 

It says, the distribution channel is not same.

Thanks

Praneeth

entry.title: Re: HANA Smart Data Integration - Data Provisioning Agent...
entry.content: direct connection is not available. If the system support ODATA connection, SDI can connect through ODATA. If you are not sure, you can report an incident to SAP under component LOD-EC-GCP-PY-OPS, confirm with the OPS team to see if there is ODATA connection available Regards Helen

entry.title: Access Control Objects
entry.content:
Hi Amigos,

I wanted to ask like in Access Control Objects, what are all clauses used?

Thank you,

kaushik

SAP Access Control for SAP S/4HANA

entry.title: How to verify duplicated value of reference field in MIRO
entry.content:
Dear Expert,

First of all, please aware that we only activate FI module in this system, we do not have MM module. So we're only using MIRO to post some good received of expense/fixed asset purchasing.

Now we're facing this issue that we usually put the Vendor PO number in Reference field.  Due to some data confusion, they might post duplicated goods received into system.

So they require a verify function, system will occure an error in MIRO once they enter a vendor PO that already posted.

Could you suggest us how to do it?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to verify duplicated value of reference field in...
entry.content:
Could you please use BADI MRM_CHECK_INVOICE_CLOUD to perform the custom check of Reference field?

You may refer to this online documentation for all available BADIs for supplier invoice:
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0e602d466b99490187fcbb30d1dc897c/5a27c5f57c3f4dffa6881ca587b22571.html?locale=en-US

entry.title: Re: bapi_po_change issue in looping when mass deleting th...
entry.content:
Did you analyze the RETURN table from BAPI_PO_CHANGE to identify any specific errors or warnings related to the deletion process.

Make sure that you are providing the correct key fields in the PO_ITEM and PO_ITEM_EXT structures. For deleting an item, you need to specify the item number and the appropriate fields.

entry.title: Re: DP Agent config tool - Exception
entry.content: Usually there won't be such error, not sure what's the dp agent version? Which user did you use to start agentcli? Please make sure the user has the full access, also try latest version of DP agent installation

entry.title: Re: DP Agent config tool - Exception
entry.content: The inforamtion you see in pic2 means you ever configured this dpagent to connect to that HANA

entry.title: Re: Multilingual content in SuccessFactors Learning
entry.content: Hi Chris, I am also looking for support on this. If we have 2 content objects per language, how do we ensure that they complete both content objects before marking the item off as complete?

entry.title: Re: I create sales order and I do delivery when I press p...
entry.content:
Hi,

It is authorization issue please use SU53 t code to find missing authorization and share the same screenshot with the BASIS team for the assigning the authorization.

Regards,

entry.title: Re: bapi_po_change issue in looping when mass deleting th...
entry.content: Thank you for reply. But the main objective is delete all BOM components under 1 item but when I used the BAPI_PO_CHANGE first component is deleted but when hits the second component to delete , it always returned "NO CHANGED"

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud - Embedded Analytics: Custom Analytical Queries
entry.content:
Overview:

In this blog, we will explore about Analytical Queries and how standard embedded analytics coverage with option to additionally enhance with Custom Analytical Queries. This is a central object of most of the Analytical use cases in S/4HANA. Custom Analytical queries transforms and organizes raw data delivered from business document into a meaningful report. SAP S/4HANA comes with long list of pre-defined queries that are released for customer usage. However, there are always a customer specific queries to provide data to answer special questions and address specific use cases. To address this need Custom Analytical Queries are created. This extensibility feature is available in both Public / Private editions.

As shown in the above diagram, Analytical Query Provides data to:

  • KPIs.
  • Multidimensional reports
  • Sap Analytics Cloud Stories/Dashboards

Analytics specialist role is required to create these queries. Creating Analytical queries and maintaining the existing queries are the main tasks of the analytics specialist. We can create Custom Analytics Queries using Custom Analytics App in the Query design business catalogue.

Custom Analytics Queries are created based on below options:

Custom Analytical Queries

Step 1: Access the Custom Analytical Queries App in S/4HANA cloud.

 

Step 2: Create Custom Analytical Queries by selecting existing Data source.

As depicted in the above diagram, Once launched the custom analytical query app, we will be presented with the above screen. In the top right corner select the create button and provide the Custom query name and underlying data source. In this example we are trying to use the existing Released cube “C_ANALYZESUPRUTILZNCUBE” and build custom query on top of it. Please note the system will automatically prefix YY1 in the query names.

Step 3: Providing Necessary Information in “General” tab.

Step 4: Selecting Required fields in the custom query.

We will have to pick the required fields for the custom query from the source data source. In this case we have selected all the fields from the source. Please note the numeric values are automatically grouped under “measure structure”. The other fields are in the “Free” Section.

Step 5: Selecting Display fields for the Custom Query.

In the “Display” tab pick the required fields as shown above. Please note, for dimensions pick the Row, so that we can analyse based on that specific field. In this example we have selected “Country” as a dimension to analyse the number of contracts from country wise. You can leave rest of the dimensions in the “Free” characteristic section itself. so that – the end user can pick and choose dynamic fields based on the changing requirement.

Step 6: Selecting “Filters” for the Query

In the Last tab – “Filter” Section include the necessary filters for the result set to appear in the report. As shown above, select the appropriate radio buttons and apply the filtering criteria.  The user also can select User input values as a filtering option. In this example we have restricted the result by excluding a particular country code.

Once all the relevant fields are filtered, click save draft and publish as shown above.After Publishing, Browse the below Custom Analytical Queries list and provide the required query name and double click to access the Query and its results.

 

Execute the Query and the Country wise Result shown as below:

 

 

In the above screen, you will find ‘Data Analysis’, if the end user wish to see the result in the form of tabular format. ‘Graphical display’ is the one which shown above. We also have Query Technical information about this Custom Query. The details of query and underlying data source are displayed in this tab.

In general, these queries are consumed by the reporting tools (i.e. SAC Dashboard) so look and feel of the output and report will change based on the Reporting Tool.

Conclusion:

In this blog, we have discussed about accessing Analytical Queries and creating the custom analytical queries. Hope this demonstrates ‘Custom Analytical Queries’ creation process in a step-by-step manner. This SAP S/4HANA Embedded Analytics feature will greatly benefit our customers to implement the dynamic requirements of their business.

Other Useful Blog Links for the Related Topics are:

  • https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/s4hana-embedded-analytics-analytical-list-page-report-step-by-step/ba-p/13738327
  • https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/s-4hana-embedded-analytics-extensibility-process-amp-custom-cds-view/ba-p/13793312
  • Refer for More details from SAP Portal Help Documentation : Custom Analytical Queries

Thank You for your time! Happy Learning!!!

entry.title: Re: RAP CDS annotation based custom action triggering aut...
entry.content: Hi @sandeep_rs,
